dinosaurs.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The tyrannosaurus makes two attacks: one with its bite and one with its tail. It can't make both attacks against the same target.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 33 (4d12 + 7) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 17). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the tyrannosaurus can't bite another target.

actions.name: Tail
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

historical_facts: Lived during the late Cretaceous Period, approximately 68 to 66 million years ago.
Had one of the strongest bite forces of any terrestrial animal, estimated at 8,000 to 12,800 pounds per square inch.
Despite their tiny arms, each arm could lift approximately 450 pounds.
Had excellent binocular vision, possibly better than modern hawks.
New evidence suggests they may have had 'lips' covering their teeth when their mouths were closed.

dinosaurs.name: Triceratops

traits.name: Trampling Charge
traits.description: If the triceratops moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the triceratops can make one stomp attack against it as a bonus action.

actions.name: Gore
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) piercing damage.

actions.name: Stomp
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one prone creature. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

historical_facts: The name Triceratops literally means 'three-horned face'.
The large bony frill may have served as protection, display for mating, or even to help regulate body temperature.
Had hundreds of teeth arranged in batteries, which were constantly being replaced as they wore down.
Lived in the same time and place as T-Rex and was likely its primary prey—and primary rival.
Recent studies suggest their horns were used for combat with other Triceratops, not just predators.

dinosaurs.name: Ankylosaurus

actions.name: Tail
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (4d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

historical_facts: Often called a 'living tank' due to its heavy armor plating (osteoderms).
The tail club was made of two large bony knobs and was strong enough to break a T-Rex's leg.
Even its eyelids may have been protected by small plates of bone.
It was one of the last non-avian dinosaurs existing before the K-Pg extinction event.
The armor was actually embedded in the skin, similar to a crocodile's scales but much thicker.

dinosaurs.name: Allosaurus

traits.name: Pounce
traits.description: If the allosaurus moves at least 30 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the allosaurus can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage.

actions.name: Claw
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage.

historical_facts: Lived during the late Jurassic Period, about 155 to 145 million years ago.
The name Allosaurus means 'different lizard', referring to its unique concave vertebrae.
May have used its upper jaw like a hatchet to strike prey, relying on speed and sharp teeth rather than pure crushing force.
Was the most common large predator in what is now North America during its time.
Evidence from 'Big Al' fossils shows these dinosaurs lived violent lives, often sustaining multiple broken bones.

dinosaurs.name: Plesiosaurus

traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.description: The plesiosaurus can hold its breath for 1 hour.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) piercing damage.

historical_facts: Technically a marine reptile, not a dinosaur.
Had a very long, flexible neck with up to 40 vertebrae, allowing it to strike fish with great precision.
Unlike most reptiles, plesiosaurs likely gave birth to live young in the water rather than laying eggs.
Used its four large flippers to 'fly' through the water, much like modern sea turtles.
The first complete Plesiosaurus skeleton was discovered by Mary Anning in 1823.

dinosaurs.name: Pteranodon

traits.name: Flyby
traits.description: The pteranodon doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) piercing damage.

historical_facts: A pterosaur, not a dinosaur. Pterosaurs were flying reptiles.
The name Pteranodon means 'wing without teeth'.
The large crest on the back of its head acted as a counterweight to its long beak and may have helped with steering.
Had a wingspan of up to 20 feet, yet its body was very light to allow for efficient flight.
Likely spent most of its time flying over the ocean to hunt for fish, similar to a modern albatross.
